ex.en: The company is facing serious financial problems.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 심각한 재정 문제에 직면해 있다.

finding.en: information or a conclusion that is discovered through research or investigation

ex.en: The study's findings surprised many scientists.
ex.ko: 그 연구 결과는 많은 과학자들을 놀라게 했다.

finger.en: one of the five parts at the end of the hand

ex.en: She pointed her finger at the map.

fire station.en: a building where firefighters and fire engines are based

ex.en: The fire station is just around the corner.

firefighter.en: a person whose job is to put out fires

ex.en: The firefighter rescued a cat from the tree.

firework.en: a device that explodes in the sky to produce bright colors and loud noises for entertainment

ex.en: We watched the fireworks on New Year's Eve.

firm.en: solid and not easily moved, pressed, or changed

ex.en: He gave her hand a firm handshake.

firm.en: a business organization, especially one providing professional services

ex.en: She works for a law firm downtown.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시내에 있는 법률 회사에서 일한다.

ex.en: Her muscles firmed up after months of training.
ex.ko: 그녀의 근육은 몇 달간의 운동 후 단단해졌다.

firmly.en: in a strong and definite way

ex.en: She firmly believes in equal rights.

first floor.en: the floor of a building at ground level

ex.en: Our office is on the first floor.

first lady.en: the wife of a country's president

ex.en: The First Lady visited the new hospital.

first-floor.en: located on the first floor of a building

ex.en: They live in a first-floor apartment.

fitness.en: the state of being physically healthy and strong

ex.en: Swimming is great for your fitness.

flash.en: shine brightly and suddenly, especially in short bursts

ex.en: Lightning flashed across the sky.

flash.en: a sudden bright light, especially from a camera

ex.en: Don't use the flash indoors.

flash.en: a sudden brief moment of something, especially an idea

ex.en: She had a flash of inspiration.

flavor.en: the taste of a particular food or drink

ex.en: This ice cream has a rich chocolate flavor.
ex.ko: 이 아이스크림은 진한 초콜릿 맛이 난다.

flavor.en: give a particular taste to food

ex.en: Add garlic to flavor the soup.

flock.en: a group of birds or sheep, or a large group of people

ex.en: A flock of geese flew overhead.

flock.en: gather or move together in a large group

ex.en: Tourists flock to the beach in summer.

flow.en: move steadily and continuously in one direction, like a liquid

ex.en: The river flows into the sea.

flow.en: the continuous movement of something such as water, traffic, or information

ex.en: There was a steady flow of traffic on the highway.

flu.en: an illness like a bad cold that causes fever and aching

ex.en: I've had the flu all week.

fluent.en: able to speak or write a language easily and well

ex.en: She is fluent in three languages.

fluently.en: in a fluent way, without hesitation

ex.en: He speaks English fluently.

flunk.en: fail a test, exam, or class (informal)

ex.en: He flunked his math exam.

flute.en: a long thin musical instrument that you play by blowing across a hole near one end

ex.en: She learned to play the flute in school.

fold.en: bend something so that one part lies on another part

ex.en: He folded the letter and put it in an envelope.

fold.en: a line or crease made by folding something

ex.en: There was a fold in the middle of the map.
ex.ko: 그 지도 가운데에는 접힌 자국이 있었다.

folk.en: people in general, especially those of a particular kind (informal)

ex.en: Country folk often know each other well.
ex.ko: 시골 사람들은 서로 잘 아는 경우가 많다.

folk.en: relating to the traditional culture or music of ordinary people

ex.en: She loves listening to folk music.
ex.ko: 그녀는 민속 음악 듣는 것을 좋아한다.

fond.en: having a liking or affection for someone or something

ex.en: I'm very fond of my grandmother.

fondness.en: a feeling of liking someone or something

ex.en: He has a fondness for old movies.
ex.ko: 그는 옛날 영화를 좋아하는 마음이 있다.

foolish.en: not sensible; showing a lack of good judgment

ex.en: It was foolish to leave the door unlocked.
ex.ko: 문을 잠그지 않은 것은 어리석은 짓이었다.

foot.en: the part of the leg below the ankle that you stand on

ex.en: She hurt her foot while running.

foot.en: a unit of length equal to 12 inches (about 30 cm)

ex.en: The ceiling is about ten feet high.

foot.en: the lowest part of something such as a hill or a set of stairs

ex.en: They set up camp at the foot of the mountain.

forbidden.en: not allowed by rule or law

ex.en: Smoking is forbidden inside the building.

forecast.en: a statement of what is expected to happen, especially with the weather

ex.en: The weather forecast says it will rain tomorrow.
ex.ko: 일기예보에서 내일 비가 올 것이라고 한다.

forecast.en: predict what will happen in the future

ex.en: Experts forecast a rise in prices next year.
ex.ko: 전문가들은 내년에 물가가 오를 것으로 예측한다.

forehead.en: the part of the face above the eyebrows

ex.en: She kissed the baby's forehead.

forgive.en: stop feeling angry toward someone for something wrong they did

ex.en: She forgave him for being late.

formal.en: following accepted rules and conventions, especially in dress or manner

ex.en: He wore a formal suit to the wedding.

formally.en: in an official or formal way

ex.en: The two countries formally signed the agreement.

format.en: the way something is arranged or presented

ex.en: Please save the file in PDF format.

format.en: arrange text or data according to a set style

ex.en: She formatted the report before printing it.
ex.ko: 그녀는 보고서를 인쇄하기 전에 서식을 지정했다.

former.en: having been something in the past but not now

ex.en: He is a former president of the company.

former.en: referring to the first of two things just mentioned

ex.en: Of the two options, I prefer the former.
ex.ko: 그 두 가지 선택지 중에서 나는 전자를 선호한다.

formula.en: a set of mathematical symbols showing how to calculate something

ex.en: Students learned the formula for finding the area of a circle.
ex.ko: 학생들은 원의 넓이를 구하는 공식을 배웠다.

formula.en: a fixed method or way of doing or making something

ex.en: There's no magic formula for success.
ex.ko: 성공을 위한 마법 같은 비법은 없다.

forth.en: forward in time, or out into view (formal, mainly in fixed phrases)

ex.en: From this day forth, things will be different.

fortnight.en: a period of two weeks (mainly British)

ex.en: We're going on holiday for a fortnight.
We were fortunate to find a parking spot.
ex.ko: 우리는 운 좋게 주차할 자리를 찾았다.

foundation.en: the basic level or part on which something is built or developed

ex.en: Good manners are the foundation of a healthy relationship.

foundation.en: an organization that provides money for a particular purpose

ex.en: The foundation supports children's education.

fountain.en: a structure that shoots water into the air, often for decoration

ex.en: Children love playing near the fountain in the park.
ex.ko: 아이들은 공원의 분수 근처에서 노는 것을 좋아한다.

fragment.en: a small piece broken off from something larger

ex.en: She found a fragment of the broken vase.
You can move freely around the museum.
ex.ko: 박물관 안을 자유롭게 돌아다닐 수 있다.

freezer.en: an appliance or part of a fridge that keeps food frozen

ex.en: Put the ice cream back in the freezer.
It's freezing outside, so wear a coat.
The frequency of these storms has increased.

frequency.en: the rate at which a sound wave or radio wave vibrates

ex.en: Tune the radio to the correct frequency.
There are frequent delays on this train line.
ex.ko: 이 기차 노선에는 잦은 지연이 있다.

ex.en: She frequently travels for work.

freshman.en: a student in the first year of high school or college

ex.en: As a freshman, she felt nervous on her first day.

friendliness.en: the quality of being friendly and kind

ex.en: We were impressed by the friendliness of the staff.
The loud noise gave her a fright.
ex.ko: 그 큰 소리는 그녀를 놀라게 했다.

frost.en: a thin layer of ice that forms on surfaces in very cold weather

ex.en: There was frost on the windows this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 창문에 서리가 껴 있었다.

frost.en: cover a cake with a sweet sugary coating

ex.en: She frosted the cake with white icing.

frozen.en: turned into ice, or preserved by freezing

ex.en: The lake was completely frozen in winter.

frustrated.en: feeling annoyed or upset because you cannot achieve what you want

ex.en: He felt frustrated when the printer kept jamming.
ex.ko: 그는 프린터가 계속 걸려서 답답함을 느꼈다.

frustration.en: the feeling of being upset because things are not going as you want

ex.en: She sighed in frustration.

frying pan.en: a flat pan used for frying food

ex.en: Heat some oil in the frying pan.

fuel.en: a substance such as gas or oil that is burned to produce energy

ex.en: The car ran out of fuel on the highway.

fuel.en: make a feeling or situation stronger or worse

ex.en: His comments only fueled the argument.
ex.ko: 그의 발언은 그 논쟁을 더 부추길 뿐이었다.

full stop.en: the punctuation mark (.) used at the end of a sentence (mainly British)

ex.en: Remember to put a full stop at the end of the sentence.
ex.ko: 문장 끝에 마침표 찍는 것을 잊지 마.

full-time.en: working the standard number of hours per week

ex.en: She got a full-time job after graduation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 졸업 후 정규직 일자리를 얻었다.

fund.cue: 특정 목적을 위해 모아 둔 돈
fund.en: an amount of money saved or collected for a particular purpose

ex.en: The school set up a fund for new computers.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 새 컴퓨터를 위한 기금을 마련했다.

ex.en: The project is funded by the government.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 정부의 자금 지원을 받는다.

funeral.en: a ceremony held for a person who has died, usually before burial

ex.en: Many people attended her grandfather's funeral.
ex.ko: 많은 사람들이 그녀의 할아버지 장례식에 참석했다.

fur.en: the soft thick hair that covers the bodies of some animals

ex.en: The cat's fur is soft and warm.

furnish.en: put furniture into a room or building

ex.en: They furnished the apartment with second-hand furniture.
The witness furnished the police with new evidence.
ex.ko: 그 목격자는 경찰에게 새로운 증거를 제공했다.

furthermore.en: used to add more information to what has already been said

ex.en: The plan is expensive; furthermore, it may not work.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 비용이 많이 든다. 게다가 효과가 없을 수도 있다.

furthest.en: at or to the greatest distance (superlative of far)

ex.en: He threw the ball the furthest of everyone.
ex.ko: 그는 모두 중에서 공을 가장 멀리 던졌다.

fuss.en: unnecessary excitement or worry about something small

ex.en: Don't make such a fuss over a little mistake.
ex.ko: 작은 실수 하나에 그렇게 야단법석 떨지 마.

gain.en: obtain or get something you want or need

ex.en: She gained a lot of experience from the internship.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 인턴십에서 많은 경험을 얻었다.

gain.en: an increase in amount or level

ex.en: There was a significant gain in sales this quarter.

gall.en: rude boldness or a lack of respect (informal)

ex.en: He had the gall to ask for a raise after being late all week.
ex.ko: 그는 일주일 내내 지각하고서도 뻔뻔하게 급여 인상을 요구했다.

gallon.en: a unit for measuring liquid, equal to about 3.8 liters in the US

ex.en: The tank holds about ten gallons of gas.
ex.ko: 그 탱크는 약 10갤런의 휘발유를 담을 수 있다.

gap.en: an empty space between two things or in the middle of something

ex.en: There's a gap between the door and the floor.

gap.en: a difference between two situations, groups, or amounts

ex.en: There is a big gap between rich and poor in this city.

gardening.en: the activity of growing and taking care of plants in a garden

ex.en: She enjoys gardening on the weekends.
Each garment is carefully checked before shipping.
ex.ko: 각 의류는 배송 전에 꼼꼼히 검사된다.

gay.en: attracted romantically to people of the same sex

ex.en: He came out as gay to his family.

gay.en: cheerful and lively (an older, less common meaning)

ex.en: The garden was filled with gay laughter.

gee.en: used to express surprise, admiration, or mild emotion (informal)

ex.en: Gee, I didn't expect that!

gene.en: a part of a cell that determines a living thing's characteristics, passed from parents to children

ex.en: Eye color is determined by genes.

general.en: affecting or including most people or things; not specific

ex.en: The general opinion is that prices will rise.
ex.ko: 일반적인 의견은 물가가 오를 것이라는 것이다.

general.en: a very high-ranking officer in the army

ex.en: The general led his troops into battle.
ex.ko: 그 장군은 군대를 이끌고 전투에 나섰다.

ex.en: Generally, the weather is mild in spring.

generate.en: produce or create something, such as energy, income, or ideas

ex.en: Solar panels generate electricity from sunlight.

generous.en: willing to give more money, help, or time than is expected

ex.en: He was very generous with his time.
The disease has a genetic cause.
ex.ko: 그 질병은 유전적 원인을 가지고 있다.

genetically.en: in a way that relates to genes

ex.en: The crop was genetically modified to resist pests.
ex.ko: 그 작물은 해충에 저항하도록 유전적으로 변형되었다.

genetics.en: the scientific study of how characteristics are passed from parents to children

ex.en: She is studying genetics at university.
He always behaves like a true gentleman.

geographical.en: relating to the study of the earth's surface and features

ex.en: The two countries share a geographical border.
ex.ko: 그 두 나라는 지리적 국경을 공유한다.

geography.en: the study of the earth's physical features, climate, and population

ex.en: We learned about rivers and mountains in geography class.
ex.ko: 우리는 지리 수업에서 강과 산에 대해 배웠다.

geology.en: the scientific study of rocks and the history of the earth

ex.en: He is majoring in geology at college.

gesture.en: a movement of the hands, arms, or body used to express meaning

ex.en: He made a gesture to show he was joking.
ex.ko: 그는 농담이라는 것을 나타내는 몸짓을 했다.

gesture.en: an action that shows a particular feeling or intention

ex.en: Sending flowers was a kind gesture.
ex.ko: 꽃을 보낸 것은 친절한 마음의 표시였다.

ex.en: They found a giant spider in the garage.

giant.cue: 동화 속 거대한 인물, 또는 뛰어난 인물·기업
giant.en: an imaginary huge person, or a very important and successful person or company

ex.en: The company became a giant in the tech industry.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 기술 업계의 거인이 되었다.

gifted.en: having a natural talent or ability

ex.en: She is a gifted musician.

giggle.en: laugh quietly in a nervous or silly way

ex.en: The girls giggled at the funny joke.
ex.ko: 그 소녀들은 재미있는 농담에 낄낄 웃었다.

ginger.en: a spicy root used in cooking

ex.en: She added fresh ginger to the tea.

giraffe.en: a tall African animal with a very long neck

ex.en: The giraffe reached the leaves at the top of the tree.
ex.ko: 그 기린은 나무 꼭대기의 잎에 닿았다.

glance.en: look quickly at something or someone

ex.en: She glanced at her watch and hurried off.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손목시계를 흘낏 보고 서둘러 갔다.

glide.en: move smoothly and quietly, without effort

ex.en: The skater glided gracefully across the ice.
ex.ko: 그 스케이트 선수는 얼음 위를 우아하게 미끄러지듯 나아갔다.

glimpse.en: a very brief look at something

ex.en: I only caught a glimpse of the singer.
ex.ko: 나는 그 가수를 잠깐 언뜻 봤을 뿐이다.

glint.en: shine with a brief flash of light

ex.en: Her ring glinted in the sunlight.

global.en: relating to or covering the whole world

ex.en: Climate change is a global problem.
We watched a glorious sunset over the sea.
ex.ko: 우리는 바다 위로 눈부신 노을을 보았다.

glory.en: great honor or fame won by an important achievement

ex.en: The team celebrated their moment of glory.

goalkeeper.en: the player whose job is to stop the ball from entering the goal

ex.en: The goalkeeper made an amazing save.
Athena was the goddess of wisdom in Greek mythology.
He is a professional golfer.

goodness.en: the quality of being kind, moral, or good

ex.en: She has a real sense of goodness in her heart.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마음속에 진정한 선함을 지니고 있다.

ex.en: Goodness, look at all this snow!

goods.en: things that are made to be sold

ex.en: The store sells a variety of goods.
She wore a gorgeous dress to the party.
ex.ko: 그녀는 파티에 아름다운 드레스를 입고 갔다.

gorilla.en: a very large ape that lives in African forests

ex.en: We saw a gorilla at the zoo.

gossip.en: talk about other people's private lives, often in a critical way

ex.en: They were gossiping about their new neighbor.
ex.ko: 그들은 새 이웃에 대해 수다를 떨고 있었다.

gossip.en: conversation or information about other people's private lives

ex.en: I don't want to hear any office gossip.
ex.ko: 나는 회사 뒷담화는 듣고 싶지 않다.

govern.en: officially control and manage a country or area

ex.en: The country is governed by an elected parliament.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 선출된 의회에 의해 통치된다.

governor.en: the person who officially leads a state or region

ex.en: The governor announced new tax policies.
ex.ko: 그 주지사는 새로운 세금 정책을 발표했다.

gown.cue: 격식 있는 자리에서 입는 긴 드레스
gown.en: a long, formal dress worn on special occasions

ex.en: She wore a silk gown to the ceremony.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 행사에 실크 드레스를 입고 갔다.

grab.en: take hold of something suddenly and firmly

ex.en: He grabbed my arm to stop me from falling.
ex.ko: 그는 내가 넘어지지 않도록 내 팔을 붙잡았다.

graceful.en: moving in a smooth and attractive way

ex.en: The dancer's movements were incredibly graceful.

graduation.en: the successful completion of a course of study, or the ceremony for it

ex.en: My whole family came to my graduation.
ex.ko: 우리 가족 모두가 내 졸업식에 왔다.

grant.en: an amount of money given by the government or an organization for a particular purpose

ex.en: She received a grant to study abroad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 해외 유학을 위한 지원금을 받았다.

grant.en: agree to give someone what they ask for

ex.en: The judge granted his request for bail.

graph.en: a diagram showing how numbers or values relate to each other

ex.en: The graph shows sales over the last five years.

graphic.en: relating to or shown using pictures, charts, or diagrams

ex.en: The report includes several graphic illustrations.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 여러 그래픽 삽화를 포함하고 있다.

graphic.en: described in vivid, sometimes shocking detail

ex.en: The movie contained graphic scenes of violence.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 폭력을 생생하게 묘사한 장면들을 담고 있었다.

graphics.en: images or pictures, especially those created by computer

ex.en: The video game has amazing graphics.
ex.ko: 그 비디오 게임은 놀라운 그래픽을 갖고 있다.

ex.en: She expressed her gratitude to everyone who helped.
ex.ko: 그녀는 도와준 모든 사람에게 감사를 표했다.

grave.en: a place in the ground where a dead body is buried

ex.en: They laid flowers on her grandfather's grave.

grave.en: very serious or important, often about danger

ex.en: The doctor said his condition was grave.

gravy.en: a sauce made from the juices of cooked meat

ex.en: She poured gravy over the mashed potatoes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 으깬 감자 위에 그레이비 소스를 부었다.

greenhouse.en: a glass building used for growing plants

ex.en: We grow tomatoes in the greenhouse.

greeting.en: a friendly word or action when meeting someone

ex.en: He gave a warm greeting to his guests.

grill.en: cook food over direct heat, such as on a barbecue

ex.en: We grilled some chicken for dinner.

grill.en: a piece of equipment used for cooking food over heat

ex.en: He cleaned the grill after the barbecue.

groom.en: a man on his wedding day

ex.en: The bride and groom danced together.

groom.en: clean and take care of an animal's appearance, or make oneself look neat

ex.en: She grooms her horse every morning.

ground floor.en: the floor of a building at street level (mainly British)

ex.en: The reception desk is on the ground floor.

grouping.en: the act of putting things into groups, or a group formed this way

ex.en: The teacher used a new grouping for the students.
ex.ko: 선생님은 학생들에게 새로운 그룹화 방식을 사용했다.

growth.en: the process of increasing in size, number, or development

ex.en: The company has seen rapid growth this year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 올해 급격한 성장을 보였다.

guarantee.en: promise that something will definitely happen or be true

ex.en: We guarantee that your order will arrive within a week.
ex.ko: 우리는 주문하신 물건이 일주일 내에 도착할 것을 보장합니다.

guarantee.en: a written promise that a product will be repaired or replaced if it breaks

ex.en: The laptop comes with a two-year guarantee.

guidance.en: help or advice that guides someone's actions or decisions

ex.en: Students can get career guidance from their teachers.
ex.ko: 학생들은 선생님으로부터 진로 지도를 받을 수 있다.

guide.en: a person who shows visitors around a place

ex.en: Our tour guide showed us the old castle.
ex.ko: 우리 투어 가이드가 오래된 성을 보여줬다.

guide.en: show someone the way or help them do something

ex.en: She guided us through the busy market.
ex.ko: 그녀는 붐비는 시장을 통과하도록 우리를 안내했다.

guilt.en: a feeling of having done something wrong

ex.en: He felt guilt after lying to his friend.
ex.ko: 그는 친구에게 거짓말을 한 후 죄책감을 느꼈다.

guilty.en: having done something illegal, as decided by a court

ex.en: The jury found him guilty of theft.

guilty.en: feeling that you have done something wrong

ex.en: I feel guilty for forgetting her birthday.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 생일을 잊어버려서 죄책감을 느낀다.

guitarist.en: a person who plays the guitar

ex.en: He is the guitarist in a rock band.

gym.en: a place with equipment for exercise

ex.en: I go to the gym three times a week.
ex.ko: 나는 일주일에 세 번 헬스장에 간다.

gymnastics.en: a sport involving physical exercises that show strength, balance, and flexibility

ex.en: She practices gymnastics every day.

habitat.en: the natural environment where an animal or plant normally lives

ex.en: Deforestation destroys the habitat of many animals.
ex.ko: 삼림 벌채는 많은 동물들의 서식지를 파괴한다.

hairdresser.en: a person whose job is cutting and styling hair

ex.en: I have an appointment with the hairdresser tomorrow.

hairdryer.en: a device that blows hot air to dry hair

ex.en: She dried her hair with a hairdryer.

hammer.en: a tool with a heavy metal head used for hitting nails

ex.en: He used a hammer to fix the shelf.
ex.ko: 그는 선반을 고치는 데 망치를 사용했다.

hammer.en: hit something repeatedly with a hammer or with force

ex.en: She hammered the nail into the wall.

hand-held.en: small enough to be held and used in your hand

ex.en: She used a hand-held device to scan the barcode.

handball.en: a team sport in which players throw a ball into a goal using their hands

ex.en: They played handball at the sports club.

handkerchief.en: a small square piece of cloth used for wiping your nose or face

ex.en: He wiped his forehead with a handkerchief.

handshake.en: an act of grasping and shaking someone's hand as a greeting

ex.en: They exchanged a firm handshake.

handwriting.en: writing done by hand, or someone's particular style of writing

ex.en: Her handwriting is very neat.
This small bag is handy for traveling.

handy.en: good at using your hands to fix or make things

ex.en: He's very handy around the house.

hang.en: attach or support something so it hangs from above

ex.en: She hung the picture on the wall.

hang.cue: 일하는 요령을 터득하다 ('get the hang of' 구문)
hang.en: the knack or particular way of doing something, especially in 'get the hang of'

ex.en: It took me a while to get the hang of the new software.
ex.ko: 나는 그 새 소프트웨어의 요령을 익히는 데 시간이 좀 걸렸다.

hanging.en: a decorative piece of cloth or artwork hung on a wall

ex.en: A beautiful tapestry hanging decorated the hall.
ex.ko: 아름다운 태피스트리 장식품이 홀을 꾸미고 있었다.

hanging.en: hung from above or leaning over an edge

ex.en: There were hanging plants all along the balcony.

harbor.en: a sheltered area of water where ships can dock safely

ex.en: The fishing boats returned to the harbor at sunset.

harbor.en: keep a feeling or thought in your mind for a long time

ex.en: She still harbors some doubts about the plan.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여전히 그 계획에 대해 약간의 의구심을 품고 있다.

hard-working.en: putting a lot of effort into work

ex.en: She is a hard-working student who studies every day.

hardship.en: a difficult or unpleasant situation that causes suffering

ex.en: The family faced many hardships during the war.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 전쟁 동안 많은 고난을 겪었다.
